name: Via Algarviana: Funcho Dam Loop
name_original: Barragem do Funcho — percurso na Rota Algarviana
description: Jog the difficult 11.9-mile Via Algarviana: Funcho Dam Loop, gaining 1116 feet with reservoir views and dam scenery.
region_intro: Jogging routes around São Bartolomeu de Messines traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, Mediterranean woodlands, and lush greenery. The region offers varied terrain, including serene lake landscapes around the Funcho Dam and tranquil riverside paths along the Ribeira de Messines. Runners can experience panoramic views and diverse natural features, from natural springs in the Fonte Benémola Nature Reserve to the scenic trails of the Serra de Caldeirão mountain range. The area provides a mix of accessible paths and more challenging routes with elevation changes. Best jogging routes around São Bartolomeu de Messines The most popular jogging route is Vale Fuzeiros — circular route (PR3 SLV), a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 48 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route offers varied terrain suitable for a steady run. Another top favourite among local runners is Barragem do Funcho — route on the Rota Algarviana, a difficult 11.9 miles (19.2 km) path. This route follows the Rota Algarviana, offering extensive views around the Funcho Dam. Local runners also love the Forneca Necropolis – Arade Dam loop from Silves, a 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes near the Arade Dam, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Jogging around São Bartolomeu de Messines is defined by rolling hills, Mediterranean woodlands, and dam reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation gains. The routes in São Bartolomeu de Messines are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 361 runners have used komoot to explore São Bartolomeu de Messines's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in São Bartolomeu de Messines?
A: São Bartolomeu de Messines offers a diverse network of nearly 50 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around São Bartolomeu de Messines?
A: The running trails in São Bartolomeu de Messines feature a varied landscape of rolling hills, lush Mediterranean woodlands, and serene lake areas. You'll encounter a mix of rugged paths and forest trails, providing both picturesque backdrops and diverse surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy run?
A: Yes, São Bartolomeu de Messines offers easy, accessible paths perfect for casual jogs or beginners. While the majority of routes are moderate, there is at least one easy route available, and many moderate trails can be enjoyed at a leisurely pace.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in the region?
A: Many running routes in São Bartolomeu de Messines pass by notable natural features and landmarks. You can explore areas like the serene Funcho Dam, the tranquil Ribeira de Messines, or the natural springs of the Fonte Benémola Nature Reserve. For a unique experience, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6115837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Demo Walkway&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a suspension bridge.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible from the running paths?
A: While primarily natural, some longer running routes can be integrated with visits to historical sites. For instance, you might find trails that offer views of or lead near the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/326759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silves Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2866555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Silves&lt;/a&gt;, blending exercise with cultural immersion.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in São Bartolomeu de Messines?
A: The running routes in São Bartolomeu de Messines are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 27 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the peaceful ambiance, and the panoramic views offered throughout the routes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region features several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1419980715/vale-fuzeiros-circular-route-pr3-slv" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale Fuzeiros — circular route (PR3 SLV)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.5 km trail offering varied terrain for a steady run.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation for a more challenging workout?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more challenging run, São Bartolomeu de Messines offers routes with significant elevation gains, especially in areas leading towards the Serra de Caldeirão mountain range. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421560664/barragem-do-funcho-route-on-the-rota-algarviana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barragem do Funcho — route on the Rota Algarviana&lt;/a&gt;, which provides extensive views and a good climb.

Q: Are the running routes in São Bartolomeu de Messines dog-friendly?
A: Many natural trails in São Bartolomeu de Messines are suitable for running with dogs, especially those away from urban centers. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in nature reserves or areas with wildlife, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options available?
A: Yes, the easier and more accessible paths along the Ribeira de Messines or around the Funcho Dam are generally suitable for families. These routes offer a tranquil environment and less challenging terrain, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in São Bartolomeu de Messines?
A: The shoulder seasons of spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for running, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. During summer, it's best to run in the early morning or late evening to avoid the midday heat, especially on more exposed trails like the Barranco do Demo Walkway.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer lakeside views?
A: Yes, the area around the Funcho Dam is particularly known for its serene lake landscapes. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19318838" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arade Dam – Funcho Dam loop from Barragem do Arade&lt;/a&gt; provide extensive views of the dam reservoirs and surrounding nature.
tips: The continuation of the route displays another necropolis, this time that of Forneca. It consists of eight graves and two sinks excavated in the rock, dating from the High Middle Ages, probably used between the 6th and 7th centuries.

The Necrópolis of Carrasqueira, also known as the Necrópolis of Vale de Fuzeiros, consists of five tombs excavated in rock, dating from the High Middle Ages and attributed to the Visigothic era. The graves are oriented from northwest to southeast and are part of the Vilarinha Archaeological Circuit, which includes other important archaeological sites in the region.

The Funcho Dam is fed by the Arade River. It was built in 1993 and, in addition to energy production, its main function is to supply agricultural production land in the area. There is a small car park next to it.

The Arade River is one of the longest in the Algarve, flowing into Portimão. The Funcho Dam, created mainly to feed the region's agricultural production, turns this section of the river into a beautiful Albufeira, which runs alongside the Via Algarviana.

faqs.question: What is the typical difficulty level for jogging this loop?
faqs.answer: This 19.2 km loop is classified as a difficult jogging route, requiring very good fitness. While some sections of the broader area are considered moderate, this specific variant includes significant elevation changes, with an elevation gain of 340 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and surface can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The loop features varied terrain. While a significant portion may consist of mostly paved surfaces, you'll also encounter reservoir-side paths and natural trails traversing surrounding hills. Expect some unpaved sections and noticeable ascents, particularly after crossing the Funcho Dam.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several points of interest, including historical sites like the Forneca Necropolis and Necrópole da Carrasqueira. The route also passes by the impressive Barragem do Funcho and the Funcho Dam (Arade River Reservoir), offering expansive water views and panoramic vistas of the tranquil inland Algarve environment.

faqs.question: Is the Funcho Dam Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails around Funcho Dam are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near agricultural land, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees to jog this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Via Algarviana: Funcho Dam Loop. It's a public trail accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The Algarve region generally offers pleasant weather for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, so if jogging then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the peak heat.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Funcho Dam Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the loop is centered around the Funcho Dam. Look for designated parking near the dam itself or at common access points for the Via Algarviana in the vicinity. It's advisable to check local maps or satellite views for the most convenient starting points.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to the Funcho Dam area are limited. It's generally more convenient to reach the trailhead by car. If relying on public transport, you might need to combine bus services to a nearby town with a taxi or a longer walk to the starting point.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities nearby, like cafes or restrooms?
faqs.answer: The area around Funcho Dam is quite natural and less developed. You'll find limited facilities directly on the trail. It's recommended to bring all necessary supplies, including water and snacks. For cafes, pubs, or restrooms, you would likely need to visit nearby villages or towns, which are not directly adjacent to the loop.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of the larger Via Algarviana network, covering 54% of the route. It also includes sections of the Via Algarviana - Ligação 12: Funcho a Vale Fuzeiros (24%), PR2 - Nos Passos do Património (12%), and Vale Fuzeiros (12%). This offers opportunities to extend or combine your jog with other paths.

faqs.question: What should I consider when preparing for the difficulty of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and 340 meters of elevation gain over 19.2 km, this route is best suited for those with very good fitness. Be prepared for a tough climb after crossing the Funcho Dam. Ensure you have appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient hydration, and are comfortable with varied terrain, including both paved and unpaved sections.
